npcs.name: Vishara
npcs.role: The First Instrument — Principle of Dissipation
npcs.location: Unknown — operating through agents and slow influence
npcs.knows: One of three Instruments. The Principle of Dissipation — works through erosion, not force. Does not possess. Does not replace. Bends what is already there. Has been shaping Jarl Mathr since the beginning. Fears what Dugweb could still do.
npcs.wants: The cage to close without anyone noticing it was built.
npcs.hides: Its full nature. The other two Instruments are campaigns 2 and 3.
threads: Works through the same methods Erik identified: attention, vagueness, slow forgetting
Jarl Mathr is its primary political instrument in Lösweg — shaped from the start, not possessed
Did not replace Mathr. Made Mathr more completely the worst version of himself.
Fears Dugweb — has ensured Mathr keeps Lösweg invisible to him during annual visits
Mathr's family appeared in Lösweg sixty years ago with no origin story — Vishara's placement
The other two Instruments are campaigns 2 and 3 — no threads pointing there yet

npcs.name: Jarl Mathr
npcs.role: Council Jarl — Vishara's political instrument in Lösweg
npcs.knows: Power second only to Jarl Beorn. Shaped by Vishara since the beginning — his family appeared sixty years ago, already positioned. Duty hardened into severity, pragmatism into willingness to make extreme choices. Arranged Shadow-Walker passage to Halvash through Aldric Vane. Has managed every Dugweb visit for sixty years. Beorn trusts him completely. Does not appear to have aged in sixty years.
npcs.wants: For Lösweg to survive by becoming harder. To serve what he believes is Lösweg's future.
npcs.hides: Nothing he is aware of hiding. Does not know the full purpose of Vishara's interest in him.
threads: His family appeared sixty years ago with no origin story — Serris flagged this. Party does not yet know it points at Mathr.
Arranged Shadow-Walker passage via Aldric Vane — told Vane only 'three passengers, quiet transit'
Has not visibly aged in sixty years — Vane has never questioned this. The arithmetic is damning.
Not possessed — Vishara shaped him by amplifying what was already there
Has managed every Dugweb visit to keep Vishara under cover
Beorn trusts him implicitly — sixty years of careful evidence
Beorn is the key: if the cover is pulled back properly, Beorn would act
The token from the end fight is Mathr's — not Vane's. It points directly at him.

npcs.name: Jarl Beorn
npcs.role: Council leader — personal ally of Dugweb
npcs.knows: Leads the Lösweg council. Deep personal relationship with King Dugweb. Trusts Mathr completely. Is not foolish. Is deceived.
npcs.wants: The security of Lösweg. To honour his relationship with Dugweb.
npcs.hides: Nothing deliberately — he is not compromised, only deceived.
threads: Not corrupted — deceived. There is a path through this.
If a real case is built, Beorn would act against Mathr
His relationship with Dugweb is the most direct route to bringing Dugweb's attention east
Mathr has kept him in the dark for sixty years — thoroughly and patiently
Vishara fears Beorn acting — which is why Mathr has been so careful

npcs.name: Erik
npcs.role: Cleric of Ezrah — architect of the network
npcs.knows: One of the five who stopped the Shade of Qadim al-Sharr. Understood the Shade was a symptom, not the disease. Built a quiet counter-network across Karsac and Exandria — never an army, a web of memory. Recognised the enemy works through attention, vagueness, and slow forgetting. Successfully moved artefacts out of Karsac to the Cobalt Soul in Exandria.
npcs.wants: To keep enough of the world awake for when the cage opens.
npcs.hides: His location. His full picture of Maharuq. The complete shape of the network — compartmentalised deliberately.
threads: Disappeared five years ago — went deeper into Karsac, still moving, still watching
The Yantravaq identified him as the network's centre and moved against him
Recruited the Cobalt monk on Halvash 28 years ago — anchor of the Exandrian network
Placed Maret in Torweg — gave impossible fragment as proof of his watching
Left the note for Pell Duvash: 'stay, and tell the truth to whoever comes'
The coded message refers to him only as 'the Cleric' — Serris does not know who this is
Maret knows his face, not his name
Network relies heavily on Veyr — the language of crossing boundaries

npcs.name: The Cobalt Monk
npcs.knows: Recruited by Erik on Halvash 28 years ago. Became the centre of the Exandrian network. Recruited the Buyer 25 years ago. Was the courier collecting items from Serris twice a year — intended recipient of the manifest. Knew he was discovered.
npcs.wants: Nothing now.
npcs.hides: Whatever he knew died with him unless the message reaches Serris.
threads: Ambushed on the road to Bysaes Tyl — almost certainly Yantravaq
Gave Erwing a dying promise: find Cumbria, reach Halvash, get the coded message to Serris
Was the intended recipient of the Pryzi manifest — his death means it never arrived
Coded message: 'Get word to the Cleric. I am discovered, trust no one, continue the work. It is now more important than ever. They are close to discovery. We must not allow them to succeed.'
Name and full history to be fleshed out later

npcs.name: The Buyer
npcs.role: Aeorian relic dealer — Exandrian network node
npcs.knows: Thirty years handling Aeorian relics. Recruited 25 years ago by the Cobalt monk. Sees the adversary's face as violent suppression of old knowledge. Does not know Karsac. Does not know Erik's name or face. Pays Cumbria a retainer.
npcs.wants: To have sent the scroll case to the right hands.
npcs.hides: Twenty-five years of varied success and loss.
threads: Under long-term surveillance by the Employer — years of suspicion, no actionable evidence yet
Sending the party east was uncharacteristically loud — triggered Davan's hire
The Cobalt monk is dead — the Buyer almost certainly does not know yet
Cumbria is on retainer — his most reliable and irreplaceable logistics asset
The other Glasswake captain is in the Employer's orbit — the Buyer does not know

npcs.name: The Employer
npcs.knows: Mid to high ranking Yantravaq agent. Has spent years watching the Buyer. Suspects the Buyer is a network node but has never gathered enough evidence to act. Arranged the Halvash theft under direct instruction from above. Has the second Glasswake captain in his network without that captain knowing. Believes Cumbria is simply a hired hand.
npcs.wants: To understand what is at play. To build a case against the Buyer.
npcs.hides: His identity. His affiliation. His growing agitation. His hold over the second Glasswake captain.
threads: Has the second Glasswake captain in his network — the Halvash route is partially watched
Believes Cumbria is out of the loop — will not move against her while this holds
The Halvash theft was ordered from above his usual level
Davan will not work for him again — may not accept this quietly
Trail back to him from Halvash is effectively cold
Will become increasingly aggrieved as the party succeeds in Lösweg
Hooded, educated voice, salt and something herbal — Davan's description only

npcs.name: Captain Cumbria
npcs.role: Captain, The Windmere — Buyer's logistics asset
npcs.knows: One of only two western captains alive who know the Glasswake. Has spent years moving letters and cargo for the Buyer — never asking what or why. Paid a retainer, not per job. Knows there is a bigger picture. Has made a deliberate decision not to look for what it might be — discipline, not ignorance.
npcs.wants: Her business, her route, her freedom to keep working.
npcs.hides: The quiet satisfaction of being trusted. Has guessed more of the shape of things than she lets on.
threads: On retainer to the Buyer — available, not hired per job, no transaction trail
One of only two people who can make the Glasswake crossing — irreplaceable
Erwing is coming to find her in Syrinlya — she is his next step toward Halvash
The second Glasswake captain is in the Employer's orbit — Cumbria does not know
If the Employer concludes she knows more than she should, her protected status ends
Sails at dawn — told the company once. Will not remind them.

npcs.name: The Second Captain
npcs.knows: One of only two captains alive who know the Glasswake. Has no idea he is in the Employer's network.
npcs.wants: His business and his route.
npcs.hides: Nothing deliberately — he does not know what he is.
threads: The Employer has access to him without his knowledge — the Halvash route is compromised on one side
Anyone the Buyer tries to move east via this captain could be watched
Name and nature of the Employer's hold not yet established

npcs.name: Davan Reish
npcs.knows: Highly skilled operative with his own moral code. Watched the party all the way through the Frigid Depths, unnoticed. Took an almost instant dislike to the Employer. Filed his report — professional and complete.
npcs.wants: To be done with this job and stay done.
npcs.hides: Whether he allowed himself to be discovered on Halvash on purpose. He isn't entirely sure himself.
threads: Filed his report — professional, complete, exactly what was contracted
Will not work for the Employer again if he can help it
The Employer may not release him quietly as the party's activities become more costly
If the party treated him fairly, he owes them one — that debt is live
Could be dragged back into orbit depending on the Employer's agitation

npcs.name: Serris
npcs.location: Halvash (Cobalt Soul archive) — shaken after the archive encounter
npcs.knows: Three years on Halvash documenting Lösweg oral histories. Knows there is a land to the east. Was holding the Pryzi manifest and key pouch for the next courier. Shadow-Walkers got the key. Manifest is with the company. Matched scroll case symbols to the Greyback document. Flagged a family with no origin story sixty years ago — this is Mathr's family.
npcs.wants: To understand what the oral histories mean. To come to Lösweg.
npcs.hides: How frightened he is. Nobody has asked him what was stolen yet.
threads: Is a conduit — small enough to go unnoticed, competent enough to be effective
The coded message is coming via Erwing — when decoded: 'get word to the Cleric'
Serris does not know who the Cleric is even after decoding
The manifest is in Veyr — partially legible to the Skald, not fully readable yet
The key is gone east with the Shadow-Walkers — to the Pryzi vault
Flagged a family with no origin story — this is Mathr's family. The party doesn't know this yet.
Nobody has asked what was stolen yet — this conversation is coming

npcs.name: Erwing Smallfoot
npcs.role: Halfling monk — promise keeper (PC or NPC)
npcs.knows: Almost nothing. Made a promise to a dying monk he barely knew. Following it faithfully: find Cumbria, go to Halvash, give the coded message to Serris.
npcs.wants: To keep his promise.
npcs.hides: Nothing deliberately. Simply doesn't know.
threads: Carries the coded message — does not know what it says or why it matters
Looking for Cumbria in Syrinlya — she is his gateway to Halvash
If PC: background belongs to the player — notes kept light
If NPC: knows nothing beyond the next step of the promise

npcs.name: Edvar Solm
npcs.location: Halvash (fixed) — came to the Meridian early morning
npcs.knows: Second-generation Halvashi. Fluent Lösweg. Knows the Buyer's letter was not routine. More frightened than he looks. Wrote the party a letter of introduction in Lösweg. Pulled every string available and found a Lösweg vessel sitting off the eastern coast with figures matching Shadow-Walker description in a launch.
npcs.wants: For Halvash to keep functioning.
npcs.hides: The depth of his fear and how much he has quietly inferred.
threads: Spotted Davan Reish and tipped the party quietly
Came to the Meridian early morning to deliver the Shadow-Walker intelligence — came himself, no message
Fixed on Halvash — his identity is the threshold between worlds
Handed translation duties to Sygna for the voyage

npcs.name: D'rek
npcs.knows: Twenty years on Halvash. Arrived as someone else. Knows more about the Lösweg quarter's troubles than she has told anyone.
npcs.wants: For the island to keep functioning.
npcs.hides: What she knows about Lösweg.
threads: Lösweg quarter quiet three months running — more than she's said
Trusted Cumbria's judgment in bringing the party

npcs.name: Floki
npcs.knows: Almost no Common. Waited six weeks on Halvash. Knows the warrior presence at their Torweg departure was not a courtesy. Saw the Jarl's token after the end fight. Worried for the company after the archive encounter.
npcs.wants: To understand what is wrong in Lösweg and get his crew home safely.
npcs.hides: His grandmother's prophecy is a fact, not a story.
threads: Grandmother's prophecy: 'when they come from the east, the thing sleeping under the Yngondi Wastes will be close to waking'
Cargo aboard the Greyback — party agreed to view it at daylight
Thrall at the Whale-Road wore Lösweg clothing — Floki recognised it, said nothing
Saw the token — said 'we should not be in Torweg when morning comes'. Token is Mathr's.
The Whale-Road question: how did the Shadow-Walkers get to Halvash? Jarl Mathr arranged it. Floki does not know this yet.

npcs.name: Sygna
npcs.knows: Eleven years with Floki. Lösweg acquired functionally. Reads Floki better than most people read their own family.
npcs.wants: Nothing personal. Does the job and watches.
npcs.hides: Nothing deliberately.
threads: Translations rougher and slower than Edvar's — occasionally wrong in ways that matter
Will flag 'that one mattered' when Floki says something significant

npcs.name: Aldric Vane
npcs.role: Under-chieftain, Torweg — loyal instrument of Jarl Mathr
npcs.knows: Age 33. Has worked for Mathr for fifteen years, as his father did before him. Has never questioned Mathr. Was told to arrange quiet transit for three passengers. Does not know who the passengers were. The token used by the household warriors is Mathr's token, not his.
npcs.wants: To serve Mathr faithfully.
npcs.hides: Nothing deliberately — he simply has never looked closely enough.
threads: Age 33, fifteen years in service — his father served Mathr before him. Mathr has been in Lösweg sixty years and does not appear to have aged. Vane has never done that arithmetic.
If the ageing question is raised directly, the cognitive dissonance is visible and uncomfortable
Arranged transit for three passengers on Mathr's orders — did not know who they were
The token from the end fight is Mathr's token, not Vane's — evidence points past him to Mathr
If the party shows him the token, a loyal man confronts the first evidence that what he serves may not be what he thought
Not a villain — a loyal man who has never needed to look closely at anything

npcs.name: Maret
npcs.knows: Lösweg-born, grew up in Torweg. Met Erik three years ago. Knows Torweg has been wrong longer than three months. Knows Vane is not what he appears but cannot prove it.
npcs.wants: To repay the debt to Erik. To survive what they've walked into.
npcs.hides: Why they left Torweg. The depth of what Erik told them.
threads: Placed by Erik — the repayment of a debt
Carries an impossible fragment proving Erik's watching
Points party toward Valweg
Knows Erik's face, not his name

npcs.name: Brix
npcs.knows: What Tor Ashfen found when he went back to Torweg. Believes the change started before he went back — happening slowly, to many people.
npcs.wants: To be believed. Needs patience, not pressure.
npcs.hides: Everything until DC 13 Persuasion or good roleplay.
threads: First glimpse of Vishara's sustained influence — slow reorientation, not possession
Ashfen still in Torweg, functional but not entirely himself
Implies it has been happening to many people for a long time

npcs.name: Tor Ashfen
npcs.knows: Went back to Torweg. Found out what was wrong. Came back different.
npcs.wants: Unknown — possibly no longer entirely himself.
npcs.hides: What he found. What is being done to him.
threads: Asks different questions, doesn't remember old jokes — slow reorientation consistent with Vishara's influence
Still in Torweg, still working — not dramatically changed, just wrong

npcs.name: Pell Duvash
npcs.knows: Three consignments of old objects, Stormwatch origin, moving north toward Yngondi direction. Received a note: 'stay, and tell the truth to whoever comes.'
npcs.wants: To get out of Torweg alive.
npcs.hides: Nothing, once he talks.
threads: The note is Erik's fingerprint — nobody names it yet
Objects moving north before the party arrived
Assassins sent by Vane's organisation on Mathr's authority
Needs extracting from Torweg

npcs.name: The Halvashi Thief
npcs.knows: Hired to steal the manifest and key pouch. Did not speak or understand Veyr. Examined the pouch — guessed at a key.
npcs.wants: Nothing now.
npcs.hides: Nothing he didn't give up.
threads: Hired by the Issylran merchant — could describe the person, not name them
Was never meant to survive — Shadow-Walkers were the real movers
Trail back west through him ends here
Skald confirmed via limited Veyr: the manifest is an inventory, not the Greyback cargo

npcs.name: The Issylran Merchant
npcs.location: Halvash — keeping a very low profile
npcs.knows: Paid by the Employer to arrange the theft. Told only what he needed to know.
npcs.wants: To not be found.
npcs.hides: His connection to the Employer.
threads: The only westward link between the Employer and the Halvash operation
Trail to him is cold unless the company actively goes looking
Issylran — operates on the eastern side of Halvash's divided community
Name and appearance not yet established

npcs.name: The Lösweg Captain
npcs.role: Unknown captain — carrier of the key and manifest
npcs.knows: Gave the manifest and key pouch to Serris on Halvash for safekeeping. Knows the Whale-Road. Has no connection to the Shadow-Walker passage.
npcs.wants: Unknown.
npcs.hides: Unknown.
threads: Unconnected to the Shadow-Walker passage — that was Jarl Mathr's arrangement
The only named link between the key's origin and Halvash — a live thread
Knows the Whale-Road
Name and identity not yet established

npcs.name: King Dugweb
npcs.role: King of Zörsdkog — one of the five
npcs.knows: Used Kurogane — meteoric steel — to kill the Shade. Now a king carrying a victory he has never fully explained to himself. Annual visit to Lösweg approaching. Has a genuine deep relationship with Jarl Beorn.
npcs.wants: Unknown currently — but Vishara fears what he could still do.
npcs.hides: The full weight of what they stopped.
threads: Annual visit approaching — Mathr has managed this for sixty years, keeping himself and Vishara invisible
Vishara fears Dugweb — he could still act, and Kurogane is not of earth
His relationship with Beorn is the most direct route to bringing his attention to what is happening
Lösweg is a vassal state of Zörsdkog — Dugweb has standing to act if given reason

npcs.name: The Yngondi Shadow-Walkers
npcs.role: Yantravaq agents — three in Ch.1, one dead
npcs.location: Departed Halvash — carrying the Pryzi key
npcs.knows: Sent to retrieve the key to the Pryzi Mountain vault. Got to Halvash via passage arranged by Jarl Mathr through Aldric Vane. One is dead. Two departed with the key.
npcs.wants: Mission complete. The Pryzi vault is now accessible to the Yantravaq.
npcs.hides: Organisation, employer, origin.
threads: Got to Halvash via Jarl Mathr's arrangement — Vane arranged the transit, knew nothing of who they were
The Pryzi key is in Shadow-Walker possession — the vault can now be opened
The manifest is still with the company — a loose end
One dead Shadow-Walker left behind — geometric markings on the skin correspond to oldest Lösweg oral history sections
Bone disc left behind — matches nothing in Serris's archive, Floki said 'impossible'
Not seen outside the Wastes in living memory
Serris is shaken by the dead Walker — the body is evidence nobody has fully examined yet

npcs.name: The Pryzi Manifest
npcs.role: Key item — inventory of artefacts sent west
npcs.knows: N/A
npcs.wants: N/A
npcs.hides: N/A
threads: Written in Veyr — partially legible to the Skald, not fully readable yet
Skald confirmed: an inventory of artefacts, not the Greyback cargo
Was Erik's tradecraft — manifest travelled separately from artefacts as verification
The artefacts are now held by the Cobalt Soul in Exandria — manifest was meant to marry up with them on arrival
The intended recipient — the Cobalt monk — is dead. It never arrived.
A fluent Veyr reader in Karsac will be needed to unlock its full contents

npcs.name: The Pryzi Vault
npcs.role: Key location — second artefact cache, Karsac
npcs.location: Pryzi Mountains, Karsac — sealed until now
npcs.knows: N/A
npcs.wants: N/A
npcs.hides: N/A
threads: Contains a second collection of artefacts — separate from the Greyback cargo
Was sealed until the Shadow-Walkers retrieved the key
The Yantravaq can now open it — contents unknown to the company
The manifest may describe its contents — but the company can't fully read it yet
Getting there without the key will be very challenging

npcs.name: Karsac Artefacts (Exandria)
npcs.role: Key item — artefacts moved west by Erik's network
npcs.knows: N/A
npcs.wants: N/A
npcs.hides: N/A
threads: Successfully moved out of Karsac by Erik's network
Now held by the Cobalt Soul in Exandria — safe for now
The manifest was meant to verify their arrival — it never did. The Cobalt Soul may not know what they have.
Their nature, origin, and significance unknown to the company — a future thread
The Cobalt monk's death means nobody in the network may know they arrived safely
